title: serratula anthelmintica
intro: Serratula anthelmintica, also known as the lesser burnet or wormweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating worm infestations, hence its name 'anthelmintica', which means 'anti-worm'. The health benefits of Serratula anthelmintica include its ability to act as a vermifuge, expelling parasites from the digestive system, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Serratula anthelmintica are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
